CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-23-2025 Set For Trial On Oct-27-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-09-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS LOSCH, EHRLICH & MEYER (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS LOSCH & EHRLICH), AND MARK MEYER'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Defendant's motion to strike is DENIED.

A party may move to strike under CCP Section 436 "any irrelevant, false, or improper matter inserted in any pleading" or "all or any part of any pleading not drawn or filed in conformity with the laws of this state, a court rule, or an order of the court." "In the construction of a pleading, for the purpose of determining its effect, its allegations must be liberally construed, with a view to substantial justice between the parties." (CCP 452.)

Here, Defendants argue that, as a matter of law, Plaintiff cannot recover emotional distress damages. The argument fails. "[A] breach of fiduciary duty is a species of tort distinct from a cause of action for professional negligence. [Citations.]" (Stanley v. Richmond (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 1070, 1086.) A client may recover emotional distress damages attributable to his or her attorney's breach of fiduciary duty. (Id. at p. 1097 [referencing inter alia attorney abandonment]; see also Knutson v. Foster (2018) 25 Cal.App.5th 1075, 1094.) Plaintiff alleges that Defendants made misrepresentations to the court that prejudiced Plaintiff's case and Defendants abandoned Plaintiff on the eve of trial. (Complaint, pars. 20, 27, 41.) The court takes these allegations as true and the trier of fact will resolve the facts.

A proposed order is submitted for the court's review and signature.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/JMQ)
